question:
Exercise 18.3 found that 43\% of CMV+ patients experienced arterial restenosis within 6 months of atherectomy. In contrast, $8 \%$ of CMV-patients experienced a similar outcome. Table 18.8 contains observed counts.
(a) Use a chi-square statistic to test the association for statistical significance.
(b) Repeat the test with a $z$-statistic $(\$ 17.3)$. Show the relation between the chi-square statistic and the $z$-statistic.
data:
Table 18.8 Data for Exercise 18.3 and 18.9. Restenosis within 6 months of angioplasty by CMV serological status.

 egin{tabular}{lc|c|}
\hline & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Restenosed } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Did not restenose } & \multicolumn{1}{c}{ Total }\
\hline
\hline CMV + & 21 & 28 & 49 \
\hline CMV- & 2 & 24 & 26 \
\hline Total & 23 & 49 & 75 \
\hline
\end{tabular}
answer:
(a) $X_{\text {stat }}^2=9.879, d f=1, P=0.0017$

With continuity-correction, $X^2$ stat, $c=8.294, d f=1, P=0.0040$
(b) $z_{\text {stat }}=3.14, P=0.0017$. Note that $z^2{ }_{\text {stat }}=3.14^2=9.86 \approx X^2$ stat With continuity-correction, $z^2$ stat., $c=2.88^2=8.30 \approx X^2$ stat, c
